verses.sanskrit:
मयाध्यक्षेण प्रकृति: सूयते सचराचरम् ।
      हेतुनानेन कौन्तेय जगद्विपरिवर्तते ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration:
mayādhyakṣeṇa prakṛtiḥ
      sūyate sa-carācaram
      hetunānena kaunteya
      jagad viparivartate
verses.translation: This material nature, which is one of My energies, is working under My direction, O son of Kuntī, producing all moving and nonmoving beings. Under its rule this manifestation is created and annihilated again and again.
verses.tithi: Śukla Pakṣa Ṣaṣṭhī (until 6:00 PM EDT), then Śukla Pakṣa Saptami
verses.nakshatra: Uttara Phalgunī (until 8:41 AM EDT), then Hasta

verses.sanskrit:
महात्मानस्तु मां पार्थ दैवीं प्रकृतिमाश्रिता: ।
      भजन्त्यनन्यमनसो ज्ञात्वा भूतादिमव्ययम् ॥ १३ ॥
verses.translation: O son of Pṛthā, those who are not deluded, the great souls, are under the protection of the divine nature. They are fully engaged in devotional service because they know Me as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, original and inexhaustible.
verses.tithi: Śukla Pakṣa Pañcamī (until 06:13 PM EDT), then Śukla Pakṣa Ṣaṣṭhī
verses.nakshatra: Pūrva Phalgunī (until 08:30 AM EDT), then Uttara Phalgunī

verses.tithi: Śukla Pakṣa Caturthī (until 07:12 PM EDT), then Śukla Pakṣa Pañcamī
verses.nakshatra: Maghā (until 09:04 AM EDT), then Pūrva Phalgunī

verses.tithi: Śukla Pakṣa Caturthī (until 07:12 PM EDT), then Śukla Pakṣa Pañcamī
verses.nakshatra: Maghā (until 09:04 AM EDT), then Pūrva Phalgunī

verses.tithi: Sukla Paksha Tritiya (until 08:58 PM EDT), then Sukla Paksha Chaturthi
verses.yogaSutraConnection: YS I.24, YS I.40, YS I.26

verses.tithi: Sukla Paksha Dwitiya→Tritiya
sankhyaConcepts: Bhakti as bridge between puruṣa and supreme puruṣa
Simple offerings as sattvic prakṛti vehicles for divine connection

verses.tithi: Amāvāsyā (New Moon) — Bhaumvati Amavasya
sankhyaConcepts: Prakṛti cyclical rest (pralaya) and activation (sṛṣṭi): guṇas in equilibrium then re-manifest by Divine will
Nirodha-pariṇāma as microcosmic dissolution: outgoing saṁskāras dissolve, restraining saṁskāras arise

verses.tithi: Kṛṣṇa Pakṣa Caturdaśī (until 09:19 AM), then Amāvāsyā
verses.yoga: Dhruva (until 06:30 AM), then Vyāghāta (until 02:26 AM next day), then Harshana
sankhyaConcepts: Prakṛti's transformations: sattva, rajas, tamas fluctuations mirroring nakṣatra/yoga changes; puruṣa as steady witness
Inner mechanism: bhakti buddhi discriminates between transient prakṛti and permanent puruṣa, increasing sattva and stabilizing antahkaraṇa, allowing divine grace to flow

verses.tithi: Krishna Paksha Trayodashi (until 01:00 PM), then Krishna Paksha Chaturdashi
sankhyaConcepts: devotion without vidhi leads to avidhi-pūrvakam (improper method)
the regulative principles (vidhi) are the guidance system for bhakti
all deities are different officers/directors in the Supreme Lord's government
worship must be offered to the Supreme Lord only to satisfy all divine authorities
keyTerms: anya-devatā-bhakta ─ devotee of other gods; hacker metaphor: someone using unauthorized plugins
śraddhā ─ faith, conviction; hacker metaphor: root access certificate
avidhi-pūrvakam ─ not according to rule; hacker metaphor: improper system call
eva ─ indeed, only; hacker metaphor: strict equality operator (===)
yajante ─ they worship; hacker metaphor: they execute the worship process

verses.tithi: Krishna Paksha Dwadashi (until 04:34 PM), then Krishna Paksha Trayodashi
sankhyaConcepts: devotion as the means to accept simple offerings
the Lord accepts offerings based on bhāva (feeling), not material value
all we possess is ultimately on loan from the divine
returning a fraction with gratitude completes the bhakti cycle
keyTerms: patram ─ a leaf; hacker metaphor: the minimal viable product (MVP) of offering.
puṣpam ─ a flower; hacker metaphor: a beautiful but transient UI element.
phalam ─ a fruit; hacker metaphor: the usable output of a process.
toyam ─ water; hacker metaphor: the universal solvent that enables all reactions.
bhaktyā ─ with devotion; hacker metaphor: running a process with root privileges and pure intent.
prayacchati ─ offers; hacker metaphor: executing the transfer of ownership.
bhakti-upahṛtam ─ offered in devotion; hacker metaphor: a digitally signed package guaranteeing authenticity.
aśnāmi ─ I accept; hacker metaphor: the server accepting a valid request after authentication.
prayata-ātmanaḥ ─ from one in pure consciousness; hacker metaphor: a clean environment build with no contaminated dependencies.

verses.tithi: Krishna Paksha Dashami until 08:16 AM, then Krishna Paksha Ekadashi (Yogini Ekadashi)
verses.yoga: Dhriti Yoga until 07:15 AM, then Shula Yoga
sankhyaConcepts: the Lord pervades all through His energy yet remains distinct
realization comes through pure devotional service
keyTerms: avyakta-mūrtinā ─ by the unmanifested form; hacker metaphor: running a process in background mode without visible UI.
mat-sthāni ─ situated in Me; hacker metaphor: processes running under a privileged user account.
sarva-bhūtāni ─ all living beings; hacker metaphor: all system processes and services.
avasthitaḥ ─ situated, located, existing; hacker metaphor: a process bound to a specific resource or namespace.
mayā ─ by Me; hacker metaphor: executed with root privileges and system-level access.
tatam ─ pervaded, spread throughout; hacker metaphor: a daemon service listening on all interfaces (0.0.0.0).
sarvaṁ ─ all, everything; hacker metaphor: the entire filesystem or network scope.
jagat ─ universe, world, cosmic manifestation; hacker metaphor: the production environment or global system state.

verses.tithi: Kṛṣṇa Pakṣa Daśamī (until 22:46), then Ekādaśī
verses.nakshatra: Bhāraṇī (until 10:45 AM next day), then Kṛttikā
sankhyaConcepts: Prakṛti's transformations: guṇas fluctuate with nakṣatra/yoga; puruṣa as steady witness
Inner mechanism: bhakti buddhi discriminates transient prakṛti from permanent puruṣa, increasing sattva and stabilizing antahkaraṇa, allowing divine grace to flow

verses.tithi: Kṛṣṇa Pakṣa Aṣṭamī (until 12:21 PM), then Navamī
sankhyaConcepts: Prakṛti's transformations: guṇas fluctuate with nakṣatra/yoga; puruṣa as steady witness
Inner mechanism: bhakti buddhi discriminates transient prakṛti from permanent puruṣa, increasing sattva and stabilizing antahkaraṇa, allowing divine grace to flow

verses.tithi: Kṛṣṇa Pakṣa Aṣṭamī (until 02:51 AM July 8), then Navamī
verses.nakshatra: Uttara Bhadrapada (until 06:54 AM July 7), then Revati (until 06:30 AM July 8)
verses.yoga: Atiganda (obstacle-removing) until 03:07 AM July 8), then Sukarman (virtuous)
sankhyaConcepts: Prakṛti's transformations: guṇas fluctuate with nakṣatra/yoga; puruṣa as steady witness
Inner mechanism: bhakti buddhi discriminates transient prakṛti from permanent puruṣa, increasing sattva and stabilizing antahkaraṇa, allowing divine grace to flow

verses.tithi: Kṛṣṇa Pakṣa Saptamī (Waning Moon 7th) - Ruled by Surya
verses.nakshatra: Uttara Bhadrapada (from 06:37 AM) - Ruled by Ahirbudhnya
sankhyaConcepts: Prakṛti's transformations: guṇas fluctuate with nakṣatra/yoga; puruṣa as steady witness
Inner mechanism: bhakti buddhi discriminates transient prakṛti from permanent puruṣa, increasing sattva and stabilizing antahkaraṇa, allowing divine grace to flow

verses.tithi: Krishna Paksha Shashthi (Waning Moon 6th) — Jul 05 04:01 AM – Jul 06 04:17 AM
sankhyaConcepts: Buddhi as sovereign discriminating purusha from prakriti
Direct perception (pratyakṣāvagamaṁ) arises when buddhi rests in its true nature

verses.tithi: Krishna Paksha Panchami (Waning Moon 5th) — Jul 04 03:10 AM – Jul 05 04:01 AM
sankhyaConcepts: Bhakti-yoga as a "kernel override" that supersedes karmic programming
Purity (pavitratā) as a top-down purification granted by divine grace

verses.tithi: Krishna Paksha Chaturthi (Waning Moon's 4th Day) - Sankashti Chaturthi
sankhyaConcepts: the material world as duḥkhālayam (place of misery)
bondage from false identification as enjoyer
the steady mind (sthira citta) as sattva‑prakṛti harmony
keyTerms: śrī-bhagavān uvāca — the Supreme Personality of Godhead said; hacker metaphor: kernel maintainer granting access based on intent
idaṁ tu te guhya-tamaṁ — this most confidential knowledge; hacker metaphor: root access to divine mainframe
pravakṣyāmy anasūyave — I will speak to the non-envious; hacker metaphor: security clearance based on intent
jñānaṁ vijñāna-sahitam — knowledge combined with realized knowledge; hacker metaphor: read-only access plus root execution
yaj jñātvā mokṣyase — by knowing which you will be liberated; hacker metaphor: segmentation fault resolved by correcting false identification
śubhāt — from misery; hacker metaphor: segmentation fault (SIGSEGV) from invalid memory access
anasūyave — without envy; hacker metaphor: absence of malicious intent (envy) as the 'password'

verses.tithi: Krishna Paksha Tritiya (12:08 AM – 01:50 AM)
sankhyaConcepts: Buddhi as sovereign discriminating purusha from prakriti
Direct perception (pratyakṣāvagamaṁ) arises when buddhi rests as witness

verses.tithi: Krishna Paksha Dwitiya (10:08 PM – 12:08 AM)
sankhyaConcepts: Om as a kernel module reconfiguring consciousness to perceive puruṣa
Liberation (kaivalya) as realizing puruṣa’s complete separation from prakṛti

verses.tithi: Krishna Paksha Pratipada (until 10:08 PM EDT), then Krishna Paksha Dwitiya
verses.nakshatra: Purva Ashadha (until 09:21 PM EDT), then Uttara Ashadha
sankhyaConcepts: the material world as duḥkhālayam (place of misery)
the living entity’s bondage to punar janma (repeated birth)
the steady mind (sthira citta) as sattva‑prakṛti harmony
keyTerms: na — never; hacker metaphor: logical NOT operator negating the possibility of bewilderment
ete — these two; hacker metaphor: a bitmask representing the two distinct paths (śukla and kṛṣṇa)
sṛtī — paths, ways; hacker metaphor: execution branches in a conditional statement
pārtha — O son of Pṛthā (Arjuna); hacker metaphor: the root user seeking guidance from the kernel maintainer
jānan — even if he knows; hacker metaphor: a process that has read‑only access to the truth yet remains unaffected
yogī — the devotee of the Lord; hacker metaphor: a process with nice=-20, running at highest priority
muhyati — is bewildered; hacker metaphor: a segmentation fault (SIGSEGV) caused by accessing invalid memory
kaścana — any; hacker metaphor: a wildcard that matches any possible interrupt or signal
tasmāt — therefore; hacker metaphor: the inevitable consequence (return code 0) of a well‑written program
sarveṣu kāleṣu — at all times; hacker metaphor: a daemon process that runs continuously without interruption
yoga‑yuktaḥ — engaged in yoga (Kṛṣṇa consciousness); hacker metaphor: a process with CPU affinity locked to a single core (the Divine)
bhavārjuna — become, O Arjuna; hacker metaphor: the fork‑exec system call that creates a child process devoted to the Divine

verses.tithi: Śukla Pakṣa Pūrṇimā
sankhyaConcepts: Transcendental nature (paraṁ bhāvaḥ) beyond puruṣa and prakṛti

verses.tithi: Śukla Pakṣa Pūrṇimā (Full Moon) — begins at 17:36 EDT
verses.yoga: Śukla (Purity) from 03:59 EDT to 04:55 EDT; then Brahma (Sacred) from 04:55 EDT to 05:48 EDT
sankhyaConcepts: The vibration of oṁ as a resonant frequency that disrupts citta‑vṛtti
Liberation (kaivalya) as realizing puruṣa’s complete separation from prakṛti

verses.tithi: Śukla Pakṣa Dvādaśī (until 15:13 EDT), then Trayodaśī
sankhyaConcepts: Puruṣa operating independently of prakṛti configurations at death
Bhakta as kernel-level process bypassing material scheduling constraints

verses.yoga: Siddha (until 02:08 AM EDT on Jun 26), then Sadhya

the living entity’s bondage to punar janma (repeated birth)

verses.tithi: Sukla Paksha Navami (until 07:09 AM EDT), then Sukla Paksha Dashami
sankhyaConcepts: manas (mind) as an evolute of prakṛti
the three guṇas (sattva, rajas, tamas) as mental modulators
abhyāsa (practice) as the method to still vṛttis

puruṣa‑prāpti (attainment of the puruṣa) as the goal of yoga
